businessModel: Software platform licensing and ecosystem lock-in (CUDA)
structuredFacts.title: NVIDIA
structuredFacts.subtitle: American multinational technology company
structuredFacts.leadParagraph:
Nvidia Corporation ( en-VID-ee-ə) is an American multinational technology company headquartered in Santa Clara, California. The company develops graphics processing units (GPUs), systems on chips (SoCs), and application programming interfaces (APIs) for data science, high-performance computing, artificial intelligence (AI), and mobile and automotive applications. Founded in 1993 by Jensen Huang, Chris Malachowsky, and Curtis Priem, Nvidia has been widely described as a Big Tech company.
Originally focused on GPUs for video games, Nvidia broadened their use into other markets, including artificial intelligence (AI), professional visualization, and supercomputing. The company's product lines include GeForce GPUs for gaming and creative workloads, and professional GPUs for edge computing, scientific research, and industrial applications. As of the first quarter of 2025, Nvidia held a 92% share of the discrete desktop and laptop GPU market.
In the early-to-mid 2000s, the company invested over a billion dollars to develop CUDA, a software platform and API (Application Programming Interfaces) that enabled GPUs to run massively parallel programs for a broad range of compute-intensive applications. As a result, as of 2025, Nvidia controlled more than 80% of the market for GPUs used in training and deploying AI models, and provided chips for over 75% of the world's TOP500 supercomputers. The company has also expanded into gaming hardware and services, with products such as the Shield Portable, Shield Tablet, and Shield TV, and operates the GeForce Now cloud gaming service. Furthermore, it has developed the Tegra line of mobile processors for smartphones, tablets, and automotive infotainment systems.
In 2023, Nvidia became the seventh U.S. company to reach a US$1 trillion valuation. In 2025, amid increased demand for AI data center hardware in the midst of the AI boom, Nvidia became the first company in the world to surpass US$4 trillion and US$5 trillion in market capitalization. Because of its market capitalization and role in major technology indexes, Nvidia has been selected as one of Bloomberg's "Magnificent Seven", the seven biggest companies on the stock market in these regards.
structuredFacts.briefSummary: In late 1992, three founders conceived Nvidia over a meeting at a roadside Denny's diner on Berryessa Road in East San Jose. Before settling on their final choice, they considered names like 'NVision'—only to find a toilet paper manufacturer already owned it. They ultimately chose a name derived from 'invidia', the Latin word for envy. On April 5, 1993, Jensen Huang, Chris Malachowsky, and Curtis Priem officially incorporated the company, beginning operations with just forty thousand dollars in Priem's Fremont townhouse. By 1995, Nvidia released its first product, the NV1 graphics accelerator chip. Yet, because Microsoft DirectX API standards did not support its quadrilateral mapping technology, the NV1 became a commercial failure.

cards.title: The Diner Vision
cards.summary: In late 1992, three engineers met at a roadside Denny's diner on Berryessa Road in East San Jose . They gathered over cheap coffee to plan a new startup focused on advanced graphics technology . The founders initially considered names like 'Primal Graphics' and 'NVision' before settling on their final choice . However, the 'NVision' name was already owned by a toilet paper manufacturer, forcing a creative pivot . They ultimately chose Nvidia, a name derived from 'invidia', the Latin word for 'envy' . This global technology giant began with just forty thousand dollars and a shared dream .
cards.readerQuestion: How did three engineers plan a technology giant with almost no starting capital?
cards.keyTakeaway: A tech giant began with forty thousand dollars and diner coffee

cards.title: The Townhouse Gamble
cards.summary: On April 5, 1993, Jensen Huang, Chris Malachowsky, and Curtis Priem officially incorporated Nvidia. The founders launched their venture with minimal capital, designing their first graphics chip inside a residential townhouse. The young startup began operations with just $40,000 in the bank. This financial fragility worsened when Microsoft's DirectX standard favored triangle primitives, rendering Nvidia's quadrilateral-based NV1 chip obsolete. Despite the looming crisis, the team managed to release the NV1 in 1995. Ultimately, incompatible software standards and depleted cash reserves nearly destroyed the company before it could truly begin.
cards.readerQuestion: What early technical and financial crises pushed the new company to the brink of bankruptcy?
cards.keyTakeaway: Incompatible software standards and low capital nearly destroyed early Nvidia.

cards.title: Thirty Days From Ruin
cards.summary: In 1996, Nvidia laid off over half of its employees to fund the RIVA 128 . This drastic downsizing followed the commercial failure of their NV1 chip, which nearly bankrupted the startup . Sega's president Shoichiro Irimajiri canceled their console contract but personally invested five million dollars to save them . This unexpected lifeline allowed Nvidia to launch the RIVA 128, selling one million units within four months . CEO Jensen Huang subsequently coined the unofficial company motto, 'Our company is thirty days from going out of business' . Extreme consolidation and a crucial partner lifeline saved Nvidia .
cards.readerQuestion: How did a high-stakes gamble and an unexpected lifeline save the company from shutting down?
cards.keyTakeaway: Extreme consolidation and a crucial partner lifeline saved Nvidia.

provenance.sentence: In 1996, Nvidia laid off over half of its employees to fund the RIVA 128 .
provenance.fact: In 1996, Nvidia laid off over half of its employees, reducing its headcount from 100 to 40, to put all remaining resources into developing the RIVA 128.

provenance.sentence: This drastic downsizing followed the commercial failure of their NV1 chip, which nearly bankrupted the startup .
provenance.fact: The commercial failure of Nvidia's first graphics chip, the NV1, pushed the company to the brink of bankruptcy and forced the cancellation of their Dreamcast contract with Sega.

provenance.sentence: Sega's president Shoichiro Irimajiri canceled their console contract but personally invested five million dollars to save them .
provenance.fact: Sega's president Shoichiro Irimajiri personally visited Jensen Huang to cancel their Dreamcast chip contract, but invested $5 million of Sega's money anyway to keep Nvidia afloat.

provenance.sentence: This unexpected lifeline allowed Nvidia to launch the RIVA 128, selling one million units within four months .
provenance.fact: Nvidia survived near-bankruptcy to successfully launch the RIVA 128, selling one million units in four months and securing its financial future.

provenance.sentence: CEO Jensen Huang subsequently coined the unofficial company motto, 'Our company is thirty days from going out of business' .
provenance.fact: Nvidia's unofficial company motto for many years was 'Our company is thirty days from going out of business,' coined by CEO Jensen Huang during this period of near-bankruptcy.

provenance.sentence: Extreme consolidation and a crucial partner lifeline saved Nvidia .
provenance.fact: Extreme consolidation and a crucial partner lifeline saved Nvidia.

cards.title: The Public Ascent of Nvidia
cards.summary: In 1993, Jensen Huang, Chris Malachowsky, and Curtis Priem founded Nvidia to pioneer graphics processing technologies. To fund the development and scaling of their advanced graphics hardware, the founders sought public capital. The company officially transitioned into a public entity by launching its initial public offering on January 22, 1999. This successful debut provided the financial resources needed to expand its GPU, SoC, and API product lines. By developing these technologies, the company significantly broadened its market footprint. Ultimately, public capital accelerated Nvidia's hardware and software expansion.
cards.readerQuestion: How did the company transition into a public entity while expanding its core hardware and software technologies?
cards.keyTakeaway: Public capital accelerated Nvidia's core hardware and software expansion.

cards.title: The Five Trillion Dollar Era
cards.summary: In 2025, Nvidia surpassed a five trillion dollar market capitalization. This dramatic surge was driven by a global artificial intelligence boom that created an unprecedented demand for high-performance graphics processing units. Today, the company's chips power over seventy-five percent of the world's TOP500 supercomputers. By achieving this historic financial valuation, Nvidia established near-total dominance over global supercomputing systems. Its hardware design and CUDA software platform created a powerful lock-in effect that solidified this market position. Ultimately, it was this surging AI demand that propelled Nvidia to its historic five trillion dollar valuation.
cards.readerQuestion: How did the global demand for artificial intelligence propel the company to unprecedented financial heights?
cards.keyTakeaway: AI demand propelled Nvidia to a historic five trillion valuation

namedEntities.name: Nvidia Corporation
namedEntities.description: An American multinational technology company specializing in GPUs, APIs, and AI hardware.

namedEntities.name: Jensen Huang
namedEntities.description: Co-founder and CEO of Nvidia, formerly of LSI Logic and AMD.

namedEntities.name: Chris Malachowsky
namedEntities.description: Co-founder of Nvidia who previously worked as an engineer at Sun Microsystems.

namedEntities.name: Curtis Priem
namedEntities.description: Co-founder of Nvidia and former graphics chip designer at IBM and Sun Microsystems.

namedEntities.name: Sega
namedEntities.description: A Japanese video game company that partnered with Nvidia and provided a critical $5 million investment.

namedEntities.name: Shoichiro Irimajiri
namedEntities.description: The president of Sega who authorized a crucial $5 million investment in Nvidia in 1996.

namedEntities.name: Santa Clara
namedEntities.description: The city in California where Nvidia is currently headquartered.

namedEntities.name: CUDA
namedEntities.description: A software platform and API developed by Nvidia that enables GPUs to run massively parallel programs.

timeline.headline: Founders Plan New Startup
timeline.description: Jensen Huang, Chris Malachowsky, and Curtis Priem agree to start Nvidia during a meeting at a Denny's diner.

timeline.headline: Nvidia Officially Founded
timeline.description: The company is incorporated on April 5, 1993, starting operations in Curtis Priem's townhouse with $40,000.

timeline.headline: First Graphics Chip Released
timeline.description: Nvidia launches the NV1, which fails commercially due to Microsoft DirectX choosing triangle primitives over quadrilaterals.

timeline.headline: RIVA 128 Saves Company
timeline.description: Nvidia releases the RIVA 128, selling one million units in four months and avoiding imminent bankruptcy.

timeline.headline: Nvidia Goes Public
timeline.description: Nvidia launches its initial public offering (IPO) on January 22, 1999, transitioning into a public company.

timeline.headline: Trillion Dollar Valuation Reached
timeline.description: Nvidia becomes the seventh U.S. company to reach a market capitalization of US$1 trillion.

timeline.headline: Five Trillion Dollar Milestone
timeline.description: Driven by AI demand, Nvidia becomes the first company globally to surpass US$5 trillion in market capitalization.

briefSummaryProvenance.sentence: However, the NV1 became a commercial failure because Microsoft DirectX API standards did not support its quadrilateral mapping technology .
briefSummaryProvenance.fact: Chapter 2: The NV1 became a commercial failure because Microsoft DirectX API standards did not support its quadrilateral mapping technology.
